primary_error.message: a postcondition could not be proved on this return path

repair_strategy.problem_summary: Both loops in selectionSort and findMin methods lack loop invariants, causing verification failures for postconditions and index safety.

problem_details.what: The while loops in both 'selectionSort' and 'findMin' methods lack loop invariants. In selectionSort, Dafny cannot prove the array is sorted and elements are preserved. In findMin, Dafny cannot prove the returned index is valid and points to the minimum element. Additionally, the lack of invariants causes an 'index out of range' error when accessing a[index].
problem_details.why: Selection sort works by maintaining a sorted prefix [0, i) where all elements are less than or equal to all elements in the unsorted suffix [i, Length). Without invariants stating this property, Dafny cannot verify the final sorted result. Similarly, findMin scans for the minimum by tracking the minimum index seen so far, but without invariants tracking 'index' bounds and the minimum property, Dafny cannot verify the postconditions or prove array access safety.
problem_details.how: For selectionSort, add four invariants: (1) 'invariant 0 <= i <= a.Length' for index bounds, (2) 'invariant isSorted(a, 0, i)' to track the sorted prefix, (3) 'invariant forall lhs, rhs :: 0 <= lhs < i <= rhs < a.Length ==> a[lhs] <= a[rhs]' to establish that sorted elements are all smaller than unsorted elements, (4) 'invariant multiset(a[..]) == multiset(old(a[..]))' to preserve elements. For findMin, add: (1) 'decreases a.Length - i' for termination, (2) 'invariant from <= index < i <= to' for index bounds (this also fixes the 'index out of range' error), (3) 'invariant forall k :: from <= k < i ==> a[k] >= a[index]' to track minimum property. Also add helper assertions in test methods.
